content:
# How to Create AI Cooking Videos That Get Views on Social Media

Cooking content is one of the most popular categories on social media, with food videos generating 40% more engagement than average. But creating professional cooking videos requires hours of filming, editing, and formatting for different platforms. AI tools automate the most time-consuming steps, letting you focus on the cooking while the AI handles the production.

Explore more tools in our [[link:/category/Video|Video category]].

---

## Why AI Cooking Videos Outperform Manual Production

Cooking videos have specific production challenges: long recording sessions with multiple takes, precise timing for step-by-step overlays, and the need to reformat for vertical and horizontal platforms. AI tools address each of these challenges by auto-editing footage, generating instructional overlays, and creating platform-specific versions automatically.

The volume advantage is significant. Successful food content creators publish 3-5 videos per week across multiple platforms. AI tools make this volume sustainable by reducing production time from 4-6 hours per video to 30-60 minutes.

---

## Step 1: Record Your Cooking Session

Set up your phone on a tripod and record your cooking session from start to finish. Do not worry about mistakes or dead time — the AI will handle that. Record in landscape mode for maximum flexibility. Use natural lighting from a window for the most appealing food visuals.

**Pro tip:** Narrate each step as you cook. This audio will be used by the AI to generate step-by-step text overlays and chapter markers automatically.

---

## Step 2: Auto-Edit with [[link:/tools/1006|ChefReel AI]]

ChefReel AI is purpose-built for cooking video editing. Upload your raw footage, and the AI auto-edits by removing dead time (waiting for water to boil, chopping repetitions), detecting key cooking moments (adding ingredients, plating), and generating step-by-step text overlays with ingredient lists.

The platform's recipe detection feature listens to your narration and automatically creates a structured recipe with ingredients, measurements, and instructions. This recipe can be exported for blog posts and recipe cards, creating multi-format content from a single recording.

---

## Step 3: Add Professional Polish with [[link:/tools/51|VEED.io]]

Use VEED.io to add branded intros, background music, and professional captions. Its auto-subtitle feature generates captions in the bold, colorful style that performs best on food content. Its music library includes royalty-free tracks that complement cooking visuals.

Add speed ramps to compress longer cooking processes — a 5-minute simmer becomes a 10-second time-lapse. VEED.io's speed adjustment feature makes these compressions smooth and visually appealing.

---

## Step 4: Create Platform-Specific Versions

[[link:/tools/1011|VidRepurpose AI]] generates optimized versions for each platform. The full recipe video goes on YouTube. A 60-second highlight version with the most visually appealing steps goes on TikTok and Instagram Reels. A 15-second teaser goes in Stories and email campaigns.

Each version is automatically formatted with the correct aspect ratio, caption style, and pacing. The AI identifies the most visually impressive cooking moments for the short versions — the cheese pull, the sizzle, the final plating.

---

## Step 5: Optimize and Distribute

[[link:/tools/1086|SocialCopy AI]] generates platform-specific descriptions with recipe keywords, hashtag research, and engagement-optimized captions. Its AI identifies trending food hashtags and generates descriptions that maximize discoverability.

Schedule your content across platforms using SocialPilot AI, and track which recipes and formats generate the most engagement. Use this data to refine your content strategy over time.

---

## Conclusion

Creating professional cooking videos no longer requires a production team. With ChefReel AI for auto-editing, VEED.io for polish, VidRepurpose AI for multi-platform distribution, and SocialCopy AI for optimization, you can produce scroll-stopping food content from your kitchen. Start with your next recipe and follow these five steps. Explore more video tools in our [[link:/category/Video|Video category]].
